display.purpose: Page and workspace titles. One per screen.

title.purpose: Panel headers, control labels, table column heads. Body size, separated by weight alone.

body.purpose: The workhorse. Default for all reading. Cap prose at 65-75ch.

label.purpose: Metadata, units, badges, timestamps. The only place uppercase is permitted.

motion.name: transition-default
motion.purpose: Tailwind's `transition` default. Color and background state changes on buttons, rows, and chips.

motion.name: transition-progress
motion.purpose: Determinate progress fills in the processing pipeline (`transition-all` on cyan bars).

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Advances the job. The brightest object on the screen and the only one that inverts to dark text.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #22d3ee; color: #020617; padding: 8px 12px; border: none; border-radius: 8px; font-family: ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 600; line-height: 1.25rem; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #67e8f9; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #67e8f9; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.4; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Present but recessive. An action that exists without competing with the primary one.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { background: color-mix(in oklab, #38bdf8 10%, transparent); color: #e0f2fe; padding: 6px 12px; border: 1px solid color-mix(in oklab, #7dd3fc 30%, transparent); border-radius: 8px; font-family: ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.25rem; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { background: color-mix(in oklab, #38bdf8 20%, transparent); } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #38bdf8; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-ghost:disabled { opacity: 0.4; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Panel
components.description: The workhorse container. Sits on the ground; never floats. Depth from tone, never shadow.
components.html:
Roof sections

Four sections detected across two planes.
components.css: .ds-panel { background: color-mix(in oklab, #ffffff 5%, transparent); border: 1px solid color-mix(in oklab, #ffffff 10%, transparent); border-radius: 16px; padding: 24px; font-family: ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-panel-title { color: #ffffff; font-size: 1.125rem; font-weight: 600; line-height: 1.75rem; margin: 0 0 8px; } .ds-panel-body { color: #cbd5e1; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 400; line-height: 1.25rem; margin: 0; }

components.name: Status Chip
components.description: Read-only state carrier: job phase, measurement state. Never interactive.
components.css: .ds-chip { display: inline-block; background: color-mix(in oklab, #06b6d4 10%, transparent); color: #67e8f9; padding: 4px 10px; border-radius: 9999px; font-family: ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1rem; letter-spacing: 0.025em; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Surface-raised fill with a hairline border. Focus shifts the border to cyan — never a glow.
components.css: .ds-input { background: color-mix(in oklab, #ffffff 5%, transparent); color: #ffffff; padding: 12px 16px; border: 1px solid color-mix(in oklab, #ffffff 10%, transparent); border-radius: 12px; font-family: ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; line-height: 1.25rem; width: 100%; transition: border-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #94a3b8; } .ds-input:focus { border-color: #22d3ee; outline: 2px solid color-mix(in oklab, #22d3ee 40%, transparent); outline-offset: 1px; }

components.name: Sidebar Nav Item
components.description: Active row marked by a Surface Lifted fill. No accent stripe — The One Rule Rule.
components.html: ProjectsProposals
components.css: .ds-nav { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 4px; font-family: ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-nav-item { color: #cbd5e1; padding: 8px 12px; border-radius: 8px; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.25rem; text-decoration: none; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1), color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-nav-item:hover { background: color-mix(in oklab, #ffffff 5%, transparent); color: #ffffff; } .ds-nav-item--active { background: color-mix(in oklab, #ffffff 10%, transparent); color: #ffffff; } .ds-nav-item:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #22d3ee; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Measurement Readout
components.description: The signature component. The number a contractor stakes a bid on: legible, unit-stated, confidence plain.
components.html: Total roof area2,847 sq ft±2% from 148 photos
components.css: .ds-readout { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 4px; background: color-mix(in oklab, #ffffff 5%, transparent); border: 1px solid color-mix(in oklab, #ffffff 10%, transparent); border-radius: 16px; padding: 24px; font-family: ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-readout-label { color: #94a3b8; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1rem; letter-spacing: 0.025em; text-transform: uppercase; } .ds-readout-value { color: #22d3ee; font-size: 1.5rem; font-weight: 600; line-height: 2rem; } .ds-readout-unit { color: #cbd5e1; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 400; } .ds-readout-note { color: #94a3b8; font-size: 0.75rem; line-height: 1rem; }

narrative.overview:
A field notebook is the least precious object on a job site and the most trusted. It is plain, durable, and legible in any light. Nobody admires it; everybody relies on it. Its virtue is that a measurement written in it can be read back six months later without ambiguity. Aernova's interface answers to that standard: the numbers are the content, and everything around them is there to keep them readable.

The system is dark and flat. A near-black navy ground (#020617) carries translucent white panels that layer without ever casting a shadow. Separation comes from a single hairline rule (#ffffff1a) and from tone — not from depth effects, gradients, or glow. This is the notebook's discipline expressed in a dark surface: ruled lines and plain paper, nothing embossed. Corners are generously soft (12-16px) so the system reads as unhurried rather than engineered.

What this system rejects, in PRODUCT.md's words, is CAD and engineering complexity: exposed technical controls, dense parameter panels, and the vocabulary of the pipeline surfaced into the UI. Aernova performs photogrammetry and shows a roofer a number. The interface's job is to make that number plain, never to advertise the machinery that produced it.

The notebook metaphor carries one non-negotiable consequence: legibility is not a preference here, it is the whole premise. A notebook you cannot read is not a calm notebook, it is a broken one. Any contrast decision that trades readability for atmosphere has failed this North Star on its own terms.
keyCharacteristics: Flat by doctrine — depth from tonal layering, never shadow
One hairline rule (#ffffff1a) does all structural separation
Small, dense, workmanlike type (14px body) — but never at the cost of contrast
Cyan reserved for measurement truth, not decoration
Soft radii (12-16px) to keep a technical product feeling unhurried

rules.name: The Readout Rule
rules.body: Instrument Cyan means "this is a measurement or the action that produces one." It is never a brand flourish, never a link color, never a decorative highlight. If cyan appears next to something a contractor cannot read a number off of or click to advance the job, it is misused.

rules.name: The Ink Floor Rule
rules.body: #94a3b8 (Ink Muted, measured 7.87:1 on the ground) is the dimmest color permitted to carry text. Anything below it is prohibited outright. This rule is currently violated in ~102 places by text-slate-500 (#64748b), which measures 4.24:1 on the ground and 3.98:1 on a raised panel — below the 4.5:1 AA needs for body text. It clears the 3:1 large-text bar, so the failure is confined to the 14px body and 12px label sizes where it is mostly used. The margin is small; the fix is a find-and-replace to Ink Muted, not a redesign. The Field Notebook is legible or it is nothing.

rules.name: The One Rule Rule
rules.body: All structural separation uses the hairline at one weight. No 2px borders, no colored stripes, no left-border accent bars. If a boundary needs more emphasis than a hairline, the answer is spacing or tone, not a heavier line.

rules.name: The Weight-Not-Size Rule
rules.body: Hierarchy between a label and its value is carried by weight and color, not by size. The system runs on two real sizes (14px and 12px); reaching for a third size to make something stand out is a failure of grouping, not a shortage of scale.

rules.name: The Uppercase Confinement Rule
rules.body: Uppercase is a label treatment only, at 12px with tracking. A measurement, a heading, or a sentence is never uppercased — it costs legibility, and legibility is the premise.

rules.name: The Two-Layer Rule
rules.body: Ground → raised (5%) → lifted (10%). That is the entire elevation vocabulary. If a design needs a third layer, the layout is too nested; flatten it instead of inventing tone.

rules.name: The Scrim Rule
rules.body: Blur is permitted only where unpredictable content sits underneath — the viewer canvas, a sticky bar over scrolling content. It must always pair with an opaque scrim doing the real contrast work; blur alone is never the mechanism. On the ground, blur is prohibited.

rules.name: The Anti-Pattern Test
rules.body: If a panel appears to float above the page rather than sit on it, the treatment is wrong. Surfaces in this system rest; they do not hover.

dos: Do use Instrument Cyan (#22d3ee) only for measurement truth and the primary action that advances the job — The Readout Rule.
Do express every border as the 1px hairline (#ffffff1a) at one weight.
Do build depth from the two-layer tonal scale (5% → 10% white) and nothing else.
Do keep body copy at 14px / Ink Secondary (#cbd5e1) and treat #94a3b8 as the absolute dimmest text on screen.
Do separate a label from its value with weight and color, never a third font size.
Do state units and confidence plainly next to any measurement — PRODUCT.md: "a number presented with false precision is a liability the contractor absorbs, not us."
Do keep the (report) route on its light paper tokens. It is a printed document handed to a homeowner.
Do render a visible focus ring on every interactive element. WCAG 2.2 AA is the stated target.
donts: Don't surface CAD and engineering complexity — PRODUCT.md's named anti-reference. No exposed parameter panels, no tolerance sliders, no dense technical control clusters in front of a contractor.
Don't put pipeline vocabulary on screen: facet, normal, mesh, RANSAC, tolerance, reconstruction. If it wouldn't be said on a roof, it isn't a label.
Don't add a shadow to a translucent panel on the dark ground. If it looks like it's floating, the treatment is wrong. An opaque paper surface (the proposal preview) is the exception.
Don't use backdrop-blur on the ground. It is permitted only over the viewer canvas or a sticky bar, and only alongside an opaque scrim — see The Scrim Rule.

Don't use text-slate-500 (#64748b) for body or label text on the dark ground. At 4.24:1 it misses AA's 4.5:1, and it breaks the North Star — a notebook you can't read is a broken notebook. Use Ink Muted (#94a3b8, 7.87:1) instead.
Don't signal an error, a warning, or a state with color alone. Always pair with text or an icon.
Don't uppercase anything larger than a 12px label.
Don't use a colored left-border stripe or a border heavier than 1px to emphasize a panel. Use spacing or tone.
Don't let Confirm Green (#10b981) mean "in progress." It means done, and only done.
Don't introduce a webfont without a deliberate reason. The system stack is a considered choice, not a gap to fill.
